summary: Frontend-only verification of PublicFooter line-art divider (NEW idsea_footer_banner.png) + brand-colored SVG social icons on desktop (1920) and mobile (390). All 6 requested checks pass. Previous iteration 31 opacity/filter mismatch is now RESOLVED.
critical_code_review_comments: PublicFooter.jsx (~190 lines) is still fully inline; consider extracting SocialIcons into a separate file for maintainability.
Line-art wrapper has a redundant nested <div style={{ width: '100%', lineHeight: 0 }}> inside the outer wrapper — can be flattened.
BACKEND URL is derived via `API.replace('/api','')` — works but a dedicated env-derived constant would be cleaner.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified via Playwright: (1) data-testid='footer-lineart' rendered between grid content and copyright (DOM order confirmed). (2) Image src=/api/uploads/idsea_footer_banner.png loads (HTTP 200, 1007141 bytes, image/png, natural 1264x848, complete=true). Desktop wrapper bbox: 1920x80, mobile: 390x80. (3) Computed style: opacity=0.6, filter=none, object-fit=cover, wrap background rgb(12,60,96)=#0c3c60 — matches footer seamlessly (no filter tricks needed). (4) All 5 social-{key} testids exist and are visible; brand fill colors visible in screenshots (Facebook blue, Twitter black, LinkedIn blue, YouTube red, Instagram gradient). Note: <path> element does not carry fill attribute because fill is applied on parent <svg> — this is expected and visually verified. (5) Copyright bar text = '© 2026 Indian Dairy Scientists and Entrepreneurs Association (IDSEA). All rights reserved.' (6) Mobile 390px: no horizontal overflow (bodyScrollWidth=390, windowInnerWidth=390). Screenshots at /app/.screenshots/footer_desktop.png and /app/.screenshots/footer_mobile.png confirm the line art blends seamlessly with navy footer background — no transparency/tile issues from iteration 31.
rca of the issue: N/A — bug fix verified successfully. The switch from transparent PNG (with CSS invert/brightness filters) to a pre-composited PNG (white lines on solid #0c3c60 background) with object-fit:cover completely resolves the previous iteration's background mismatch and opacity/filter deviation issues.
